name: MOST CAPL Functions
title: MOST CAPL Functions
description: Transmission and Receipt Settings of Nodes
The registries of the Application Socket may be read-out during the entire measurement cycle.
For this purpose CAPL provides the following functions:
The 'regsel' parameter is used to select the desired registry:

Please note: The Bus Registry is created in each device during MOST system configuration. It is only valid during network status 'ConfigOk'. Generally the NetworkMaster administers the Central Bus Registry. The Network Slaves mirror it in their De-Central Bus Registry.
Changes to registries are signaled in CAPL by the OnMostAsRegistry() event procedure.
If more than one MOST channel is allocated to a CAPL node (MOST-MOST gateway) the bus context must be defined before Application Socket functions are used.
Changes the ring scan behavior of CANoe’s NetworkMaster.
Provide access to the timing parameters of CANoe’s NetworkMaster implementation.

Checks whether the functional MOST address {fblockID, instID} matches the CAPL node function blocks.

The registries of the Application Socket may be read-out during the entire measurement cycle. For this purpose CAPL provides the following functions:
Returns the number of entries in the desired registry.
Returns the logical node address of a specific entry in the desired registry.
Returns the FBlockID of a specific entry in the desired registry.
Returns the InstID of a specific entry in the desired registry.

The 'regsel' parameter is used to select the desired registry: regsel = 1: Local FBlockList regsel = 2: Bus Registry Please note: The Bus Registry is created in each device during MOST system configuration. It is only valid during network status 'ConfigOk'. Generally the NetworkMaster administers the Central Bus Registry. The Network Slaves mirror it in their De-Central Bus Registry. Changes to registries are signaled in CAPL by the OnMostAsRegistry() event procedure. Example

Sets the timeout duration, after which the PowerMaster tries to wake-up the ring after an over-temperature shutdown.
If more than one MOST channel is allocated to a CAPL node (MOST-MOST gateway) the bus context must be defined before Application Socket functions are used. See SetBusContext() regarding this.

Is called up as soon as a block from a MOST High connection has been fully transmitted.

Is called when a critical unlock is detected.
Is called when the state of the Electrical Control Line changed.
Is called before the beginning and after the end of a sequence on the Electrical Control Line.
Is called when the group address changed.
Is called when the MAC address changed.
Is called when the value of the MPR register changed (network change event (NCE)).

Is called when the node address changed.
Is called when the value of node position register changed.
Is called in response to read or write requests to registers of a MOST hardware interface chip.
Is called when the value of the synchronous bandwidth control register changed.
Is called each time the state of the Shutdown flag changes.
Is called each time when the shutdown reason changes.
Is called when stable lock is detected.
Is called before the beginning and after the end of the stress generation.

Is called each time the state of the System-Lock flag changes.
Is called if the timing mode of the MOST hardware has been changed.
Is called as soon as the light at the output goes on/off.
Is called when the NetOn / InitReady event (first "Stable Lock" after he loop has been woken up) has occurred.
Is called when the network status is changed.
Is called when the InstID of the associated function block changes.
Is called when the Local FBlockList or Bus Registry is changed.

The MOST sending and receiving behavior of the node is strictly based on the configuration in the Simulation Setup.
